section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [IMPORTANT] Error restock in vendor-stockkeeper-api
heading: [IMPORTANT] Error restock in vendor-stockkeeper-api
text:
This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock
This error can be generated by any really rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem.

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [IMPORTANT] Error restock in vendor-stockkeeper-api
heading: [IMPORTANT] Error restock in vendor-stockkeeper-api
text: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following:

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [IMPORTANT] Error restock in vendor-stockkeeper-api
heading: [IMPORTANT] Error restock in vendor-stockkeeper-api
text:
1. Identify in the **vendor-stockkeeper-api** logs which item could not be restocked correctly and determine the reason for this.
2. Resolve the issue.
3. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version.

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [IMPORTANT] Stockkeeper-api - Error restocking item (negative quantity)
heading: [IMPORTANT] Stockkeeper-api - Error restocking item (negative quantity)
text:
This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock publications and this publication has negative quantity
This case does not have a specific process defined as it is a very rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem.

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [IMPORTANT] Stockkeeper-api - Error restocking item (negative quantity)
heading: [IMPORTANT] Stockkeeper-api - Error restocking item (negative quantity)
text: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following:

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [IMPORTANT] Stockkeeper-api - Error restocking item (negative quantity)
heading: [IMPORTANT] Stockkeeper-api - Error restocking item (negative quantity)
text:
1. Identify in the **vendor-stockkeeper-api** logs which item is associated with the negative restock attempt.
2. Notify to **Envio Pack** that the restock could not be completed correctly.
3. Proceed to identify what happened (why a restock is executed that leaves the available quantities below 0).
4. Resolve the issue. 
5. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version.

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [LOW PRIORITY] Validation stock (publication has more stock than warehouse's stock
heading: [LOW PRIORITY] Validation stock (publication has more stock than warehouse's stock
text:
This alert is to verify that item was correctly paused.
It happens when something goes wrong while trying to sell a publication without stock into 3PL facilities

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [LOW PRIORITY] Validation stock (publication has more stock than warehouse's stock
heading: [LOW PRIORITY] Validation stock (publication has more stock than warehouse's stock
text: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following:

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > [LOW PRIORITY] Validation stock (publication has more stock than warehouse's stock
heading: [LOW PRIORITY] Validation stock (publication has more stock than warehouse's stock
text:
1. You should review the **vendor-stockkeeper-api** logs with the **stream-prod** scope to easily identify the item and SKU associated with the potential out-of-stock sale.
2. You should confirm that the item was correctly paused by **checking the item's status**. You need to use api core item endpoint with item_id and check "status" If the item was not correctly paused, you should raise a ticket in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out-of-stock sales.
3. Additionally, you should identify the item to monitor it in case of future out-of-stock sales.

PS: This alert is just to check item was correctly paused, it may be works automatically

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > Validation stock ERROR updating item
heading: Validation stock ERROR updating item
text: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to update the quantity of stock into the publication

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > Validation stock ERROR updating item
heading: Validation stock ERROR updating item
text: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following:

section_path: Alerts > How to resolve > Validation stock ERROR updating item
heading: Validation stock ERROR updating item
text:
1. Identify in the **vendor-stockkeeper-api** logs the item whose status could not be updated and determine why it could not be done (e.g., due to a status that does not allow updating the item such as under\_review, deleted...).
2. If the item has available units and the status allows sales without stock (any status other than deleted), a ticket should be raised in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out-of-stock sales.

text:
```plantuml
@startuml

participant OrdersNewsRouter as OrdersNewsRouter
participant ValidationRestockOrderNews as ValidationRestockOrderNews
participant ItemAdapter as ItemAdapter
participant GetStockBySkuAdapter as GetStockBySkuAdapter  
participant "API Items" as API_Items

Activate ValidationRestockOrderNews
ValidationRestockOrderNews -> ItemAdapter : get(itemId)
ValidationRestockOrderNews <-- ItemAdapter : item
ValidationRestockOrderNews -> GetStockBySkuAdapter : apply(siteId, sapSku)
ValidationRestockOrderNews <-- GetStockBySkuAdapter : product
ValidationRestockOrderNews -> ValidationRestockOrderNews : stockBelowTolerance
ValidationRestockOrderNews -> ItemAdapter : updateItemStatusAsSystem("paused")
ItemAdapter -> API_Items : PUT /items/{itemId}\nX-Seller-No-Intent\n{status: "paused"}
API_Items --> ItemAdapter : 200 ok
ItemAdapter --> ValidationRestockOrderNews
Deactivate ValidationRestockOrderNews

@enduml
```

section_path: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows > Inventory Flows
heading: Inventory Flows
text:
Before executing any action on the inventory flows, it is crucial to ensure that both the inbound and outbound
movement contain all their test items. This check is fundamental to avoid affecting the item API and, therefore, the inventory.
API and, therefore, in the inventory.

There are two main flows where this verification is necessary:

1. **Restock**
2. **Feed**

section_path: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows > Verification Procedure
heading: Verification Procedure
text:
- **Error Prevention**: Lack of test items can lead to inventory errors.
- **API Integrity**: Ensuring that the data sent to the item API is correct is essential to maintain the integrity of the system.
- **Avoiding Unwanted Alterations**: Incorrect movement can directly affect the inventory status, leading to discrepancies and operational problems.

1. **Review the Inbound (Restock flow)**: Make sure that all test items are present.
2. **Check the Operation Id (Feed flow)**: Confirm that the operationId also contains all items required.
3. **Final Validation**: Before proceeding with any action, perform a final validation of both flows.

section_path: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows > How can we do it? > Restock Flow
heading: Restock Flow
text:
Implementing this systematic verification is key to maintaining the integrity of the inventory and item API.
Be sure to follow these steps before executing any action on inventory flows.

Before we send any kind of inbound we must first get that inbound and go to inbound conciliation to check if the items have the tags that identify an item as a test item.

```bash
curl --location 'https://1p-inbounds-stage.techzoneoffice.com/inbounds/900000734/conciliations?scope=stage'
```

heading: Feed Flow
About
text: Stockkeeper-api is the application responsible for updating the stock of items belonging to the Standard area that are offered on TechZone. There are two main endpoints to perform restocking: one for 3PL stock movements (smaller quantities) and another for inbound movements, which can contain larger quantities for updating.

heading: Use cases
text:
Fresh products API documentation - sequence diagrams

POST /fresh-products/inboundorder

```mermaid
    sequenceDiagram
        participant M as Manager
        participant C as FreshProductsController
        participant S as BatchOrderService
        participant R as OrderRepository
        participant D as Database
    
        M->>C: POST /inboundorder
        C-->>S: addNewBatchOfProducts(order)
        S->>R: save(newOrder)
        R->>D: insert(order)
        D-->>R: void
        R-->>S: void
        alt Inbound Order
            S-->>C: true
            C-->>M: HTTP 201
        else
            S-->>C: OrderAlreadyExistsException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        else
            S-->>C: InvalidSectionException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        else
            S-->>C: BatchQuantityExceedsSectionQuatityException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        end
```

PUT /fresh-products/inboundorder

```mermaid
    sequenceDiagram
        participant M as Manager
        participant C as FreshProductsController
        participant S as BatchOrderService
        participant R as OrderRepository
        participant D as Database
    
        M->>C: PUT /inboundorder
        C-->>S: updateBatchOfProducts(order)
        S->>R: save(newOrder)
        R->>D: insert(order)
        D-->>R: void
        R-->>S: void
        alt Inbound Order
            S-->>C: true
            C-->>M: HTTP 201
        else
            S-->>C: OrderNotExistException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        else
            S-->>C: InvalidSectionException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        else
            S-->>C: BatchQuantityExceedsSectionQuatityException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        end
```

heading: Use cases
text:
GET /fresh-products

```mermaid
    sequenceDiagram
        participant M as Manager
        participant C as FreshProductsController
        participant S as PurchaseOrderService
        participant R as ProductRepository
        participant D as Database
    
        M->>C: GET /fresh-products
        C-->>S: getProductList()
        S->>R: findAllInStock()
        R->>D: select(products)
        D-->>R: List<Product>
        R-->>S: List<Product>
        alt List Products
            S-->>C: List<ProductDTO>
            C-->>M: HTTP 200 - List<ProductDTO>
        else
            S-->>C: ProductListEmptyException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        end 
```

GET /fresh-products/list-category{category}

```mermaid
    sequenceDiagram
        participant M as Manager
        participant C as FreshProductsController
        participant S as PurchaseOrderService
        participant R1 as ProductRepository
        participant R2 as ProductTypesRepository
        participant D as Database
    
        M->>C: GET /fresh-products/list-category{category}
        C->>S: getProductListForCategory(category)
        S->>R1: findByCategoryInStock(category)
        R1->>D: select(products)
        D-->>R1: List<Product>
        R1-->>S: List<Product>
        S->>R2: findById(category)
        R2->>D: select(category)
        D-->>R2: ProductTypes
        R2-->>S: ProductTypes

        alt List Products
            S-->>C: List<ProductDTO>
            C-->>M: HTTP 200 - List<ProductDTO>
        else
            S-->>C: ProductTypeNotFoundException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        else
            S-->>C: ProductListEmptyException
            C-->>M: HTTP 404
        end
```

text:
POST /orders

```mermaid
    sequenceDiagram
        participant M as Manager
        participant C as FreshProductsController
        participant S as PurchaseOrderService
        participant R as ProductRepository

```
